headline: xAI/Grok floor confirmation. Take It Down Act enforcement threshold now active May 2026 — FTC can impose civil penalties for noncompliance with NCII takedown requirements. 35 state AG investigation coalition ongoing; California AG cease-and-desist active; EU formal investigative proceedings active. No compliance certification, remediation disclosure, or structural harm-reduction evidence in window. Floor maintained.
key_evidence: Take It Down Act enforcement threshold now active May 2026 — FTC civil penalty authority for NCII takedown noncompliance.
35 state AG investigation coalition ongoing; California AG cease-and-desist order active.
EU formal investigative proceedings into Grok image generation ongoing.
Regulators in 8+ countries confirmed action against X/xAI.
No compliance certification, no structural remediation disclosure, no harm-reduction evidence in window.

evidence.finding: Collapse-of-accountability framing of Grok governance posture; multi-jurisdiction enforcement landscape mapped.
Multi-regulator action against Grok and X — fragmented but extensive.

scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Take It Down Act enforcement: FTC civil penalty authority active without xAI compliance certification.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). B3 Scope Clarity: noncompliance with NCII takedown across multiple jurisdictions; B5 Consent Orientation floor sustained.

negativeSignals: Take It Down Act enforcement now active without compliance certification; 35-state AG coalition; California AG cease-and-desist; EU formal proceedings; 8+ countries acting.
sector_context: xAI/Grok and Clearview AI together represent the highest-jurisdiction-count enforcement landscape in the AI Labs sector. Take It Down Act activation adds FTC federal penalty authority to the existing state and EU pressure stack.
open_actions_resolved: Take It Down Act enforcement activation captured under BND/ACC notation
open_actions_carried: Floor exit criteria require: compliance certification, structural NCII takedown program, independent harm-reduction evidence — none in trajectory
Monitor for first FTC civil penalty action under Take It Down Act
